evidence.span_text: X-rays of the left shoulder, 3 views obtained on today's visit show no obvious signs of fracture.

caps.proposition_text: Ms. Anderson has a surgical history significant for prior left upper extremity surgery.

caps.proposition_text: The clinician assesses left shoulder pain.
Ms. Anderson has a gymnastics injury.

evidence.span_text: She has a surgical history significant for prior left upper extremity surgery.
Examination of the left shoulder shows pain with range of motion.

caps.proposition_text: The clinician recommends follow-up in three to four weeks.

evidence.span_text: At this point, I discussed the diagnosis and treatment options with the patient. I have recommended taking ibuprofen 600 mg three times per day for one week. If the patient does not have relief from the ibuprofen she will take Aleve. The patient may also utilize ice or heat packs. She should rest her left arm and may not lift or carry heavy objects with her left arm. The patient should follow up in three to four weeks if the pain persists. We may repeat the left shoulder x-ray at that time.

caps.proposition_text: The clinician recommends repeating the left shoulder x-ray.
